How Are the Effectiveness and Dependability of Commercial Motors Assessed?
paragraphs: In the global economy, industrial electric motors power a wide variety of applications. The automobile industry is responsible for up to 70% of all energy used in industrial settings, 35% of all energy used in the commercial and service sectors, and 45% of all electricity produced globally, according to the International Energy Agency (IEA). Increasing energy efficiency is the cleanest, most cost-effective, and fastest way to provide reliable electricity. Motors represent around 46% of all electricity consumed and 69% of total energy used in the industrial sector. Energy consumption is one of the largest challenges facing many organizations.
